Determines whether overlapping symbols in the same layer are rendered in the order that they appear in the data source or by their y-position relative to the viewport. To control the order and prioritization of symbols otherwise, use
Default value: “auto”.
Distance that the icon’s anchor is moved from its original placement. Positive values indicate right and down, while negative values indicate left and up.

Offset distance of text from its anchor. Positive values indicate right and down, while negative values indicate left and up. If used with text-variable-anchor, input values will be taken as absolute values. Offsets along the x- and y-axis will be applied automatically based on the anchor position.

An icon or a text label.
If true, icons will display without their corresponding text when the text collides with other symbols and the icon does not.
Default value: false.
In combination with
, determines the rotation behavior of the individual glyphs forming the text.
Default value: “auto”.

The property allows control over a symbol’s orientation. Note that the property values act as a hint, so that a symbol whose language doesn’t support the provided orientation will be laid out in its natural orientation. Example: English point symbol will be rendered horizontally even if array value contains single ‘vertical’ enum value. For symbol with point placement, the order of elements in an array define priority order for the placement of an orientation variant. For symbol with line placement, the default text writing mode is either [‘horizontal’, ‘vertical’] or [‘vertical’, ‘horizontal’], the order doesn’t affect the placement.
Transition property for
Controls the frame of reference for
Default value: “map”.
Transition options for

The property allows control over a symbol’s orientation. Note that the property values act as a hint, so that a symbol whose language doesn’t support the provided orientation will be laid out in its natural orientation. Example: English point symbol will be rendered horizontally even if array value contains single ‘vertical’ enum value. For symbol with point placement, the order of elements in an array define priority order for the placement of an orientation variant. For symbol with line placement, the default text writing mode is either [‘horizontal’, ‘vertical’] or [‘vertical’, ‘horizontal’], the order doesn’t affect the placement.

The property allows control over a symbol’s orientation. If true, the symbols will not cross tile edges to avoid mutual collisions. Recommended in layers that don’t have enough padding in the vector tile to prevent collisions, or if it is a point symbol layer placed after a line symbol layer. When using a client that supports global collision detection, like Mapbox GL JS version 0.42.0 or greater, enabling this property is not needed to prevent clipped labels at tile boundaries.
Default value: false.

Default value: 1. Minimum value: 0. The unit of iconSize is in factor of the original icon size.
Label placement relative to its geometry.
Default value: “point”.
The opacity at which the icon will be drawn.
Default value: 1. Value range: [0, 1]
If true, other symbols can be visible even if they collide with the icon.
Default value: false.
The color of the text’s halo, which helps it stand out from backgrounds.
Default value: “rgba(0, 0, 0, 0)”.
Position symbol on buildings (both fill extrusions and models) rooftops. In order to have minimal impact on performance, this is supported only when

is not zoom-dependent and remains unchanged. For fading in buildings when zooming in, fill-extrusion-vertical-scale should be used and symbols would raise with building rooftops. Symbols are sorted by elevation, except in cases when

are applied.
Default value: false.
Label placement relative to its geometry.
Default value: “point”.
Note! This is an experimental feature. It can be changed or removed in future versions.
Selects the base of symbol-elevation.
Default value: “ground”.
If true, other symbols can be visible even if they collide with the icon.
Default value: false.
In combination with
, determines the rotation behavior of icons.
Default value: “auto”.
Text leading value for multi-line text.
Default value: 1.2. The unit of textLineHeight is in ems.

If true, the symbols will not cross tile edges to avoid mutual collisions. Recommended in layers that don’t have enough padding in the vector tile to prevent collisions, or if it is a point symbol layer placed after a line symbol layer. When using a client that supports global collision detection, like Mapbox GL JS version 0.42.0 or greater, enabling this property is not needed to prevent clipped labels at tile boundaries.
Default value: false.
Name of a source description to be used for this layer.

Offset distance of icon from its anchor. Positive values indicate right and down, while negative values indicate left and up. Each component is multiplied by the value of
to obtain the final offset in pixels. When combined with
the offset will be as if the rotated direction was up.
Default value: [0,0].
Text leading value for multi-line text.
Default value: 1.2. The unit of textLineHeight is in ems.
The maximum line width for text wrapping.
Default value: 10. Minimum value: 0. The unit of textMaxWidth is in ems.
